projects.name: Oliver Sports Park Synthetic Turf Replacement
projects.scope: Replacement of synthetic turf at two soccer fields, new 20-ft tall netting fence along canal, adjacent picnic area upgrades, and conversion of sports field, parking, and pathway lights to LED.

projects.name: Outdoor Fitness Courts
projects.scope: Three free outdoor fitness courts at Eden Greenway, Tennyson Park, and Alden E. Oliver Sports Park. Each court supports 28 simultaneous users on 30 equipment pieces.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 1
projects.scope: Renovations to 17 sports courts at Alden E. Oliver, Weekes, Bay Trees, and Stonybrook Parks. Basketball, tennis, pickleball, and flexible multi-use court upgrades.

projects.name: Ashland-East 14th Street - Ashland Grove
projects.scope: New neighborhood park (Ashland Grove) on 1.77-acre site. Features: multi-use lawn, active zone (sports courts/event plaza), play area, large shade trees, sensory plantings, water feature, climbable tactile wall. Awarded $3,997,500 LWCF grant + $883,914 ECRG grant.

projects.name: Ashland-Mateo Street - future Ashland Grove
projects.scope: New 1.43-acre neighborhood park designed with Trust for Public Land. Awarded $7,082,000 Prop 68 Round 4 grant. Features: trees, terraced seating, looped walking path, play areas, multipurpose court, fitness stations, lighting. Construction to begin 2026.

projects.name: Ashland Zocalo
projects.scope: Community park and plaza at 166th Ave & East 14th St developed with Alameda County and Trust for Public Land. Features: flexible community space, multipurpose lawn, play areas, fitness court, interactive fountain, outdoor stage, open-air market structure, food truck accommodations. Completed July 2024.

projects.name: Bay Trees ADA Improvements
projects.scope: Improvements to pathways and parking lots to meet current ADA standards. Construction began January 2026.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 1
projects.scope: Renovations to 17 sports courts at Alden E. Oliver, Weekes, Bay Trees, and Stonybrook Parks. Basketball, tennis, pickleball, and flexible multi-use court upgrades.

projects.name: Playground Renovations (District-Wide)
projects.scope: Renovation of playgrounds at multiple sites district-wide, including Bechtel Mini Park, Birchfield Park, Castro Valley Community Park, Cherryland Park, Del Ray Park, Eden Greenway at Berry, Eldridge Park, Greenridge Park, Hillcrest Knolls Park, Ruus Park, Stonybrook Park.

projects.name: Bidwell Park and Community Center
projects.scope: Transformation of former Bidwell Elementary School into a community hub. Features: adaptive reuse of two buildings as community center, expanded play area, walking paths, multi-use sports court, bocce courts, event plaza, dog areas, picnic areas, edible gardens, multi-use lawn. Construction to begin early 2026.

projects.name: Playground Renovations (District-Wide)
projects.scope: Renovation of playgrounds at multiple sites district-wide, including Bechtel Mini Park, Birchfield Park, Castro Valley Community Park, Cherryland Park, Del Ray Park, Eden Greenway at Berry, Eldridge Park, Greenridge Park, Hillcrest Knolls Park, Ruus Park, Stonybrook Park.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 3
projects.scope: Court renovations at Mervyn Morris Park (tennis) and Birchfield Park, Christian Penke Park, Deerview Park, Eden Greenway at Contessa, Eden Greenway at Cypress, Five Canyons Park, La Placita Park, Nuestro Parquecito, Ridge Trail Park (basketball).

projects.name: Canyon Middle School Sports Fields
projects.scope: Partnership with Castro Valley USD to improve existing soccer and baseball fields at Canyon Middle School.

projects.name: D & Clay Street Acquisition - Carol Pereira Park
projects.scope: Acquisition and development of 1.83-acre parcel. Combined with 1350 E Street parcel to create future 4-acre Carol Pereira Park. Awarded $1.3M LWCF grant and $597K Habitat Conservation Fund grant.

projects.name: 1350 E Street Acquisition - Carol Pereira Park
projects.scope: Acquisition of 2.42 acres of new parkland. Together with D & Clay Street acquisition, provides the future 4-acre Carol Pereira Park and a critical link in the Foothill Trail.

projects.name: Picnic Area Upgrades - Castro Valley & Weekes Parks
projects.scope: Renovation of six group picnic facilities at Castro Valley Community Park and four at Weekes Community Park. New paving, signage, trees, waste receptacles, picnic tables, benches, and BBQ grills.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 2
projects.scope: Renovations to 16 basketball courts and 2 tennis courts across 15 parks including Castro Valley, Canyon View, Greenridge, Nuestro Parquecito, Hillcrest Knolls, and Mt. Eden Parks.

projects.name: Tennis Court Renovations (Phase 1 / Pickleball Conversions)
projects.scope: Castro Valley Community Park: 3 tennis courts renovated. Southgate Park: 2 tennis courts converted to 6 pickleball courts.

projects.name: East Avenue Park Renovation
projects.scope: Renovation of 2-acre portion of neighborhood park: new restroom building, basketball court renovation, new play area, picnic facilities, parking lot, ADA walkways, planting/irrigation upgrades.

projects.name: Edendale Park Renovation
projects.scope: Renovation of 1-acre park: replacement of play equipment, new site furnishings, shade structure, drinking fountain, fencing, signage, paving upgrades, planting and irrigation.

projects.name: Edendale Park Restroom Replacement
projects.scope: Demolition of existing restroom and installation of new public restroom building and ADA access improvements.

projects.name: El Rancho Verde Park
projects.scope: Board-approved master plan includes: major and minor baseball fields, T-ball field, central plaza, scoreboards, batting cages, sports lighting, bleachers, dugouts, outfield viewing lawn, picnic areas, new restroom/snack bar, children's play area.

projects.name: 541 Blanche Street Acquisition
projects.scope: Acquisition of existing 90-foot baseball field from New Haven USD directly adjacent to El Rancho Verde Park.

projects.name: Fairmont Terrace Park Renovation & Expansion
projects.scope: Design and construction expanding park from 1.67 to 5 acres. Includes: ADA parking, new restroom, two playgrounds, half basketball court, group picnic areas, small and large dog parks, open lawn, pathways, hillside trail.

projects.name: Greenridge Park Renovation
projects.scope: Park improvements including basketball court resurfacing and ADA parking lot improvements (completed 2025). Playground equipment to be replaced in 2026.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 2
projects.scope: Renovations to 16 basketball courts and 2 tennis courts across 15 parks including Castro Valley, Canyon View, Greenridge, Nuestro Parquecito, Hillcrest Knolls, and Mt. Eden Parks.

projects.name: Hayward Area Senior Center Renovation
projects.scope: Renovation and modernization of Hayward Area Senior Center. Designed by Kava Massih Architects. Officially reopened June 24, 2022. Design Award Winner.

projects.name: Hayward Community Garden
projects.scope: Master plan and Phase 1: 70 garden plots with irrigation, raised beds, small orchard, shade structure, produce counter, seed library, demonstration garden, greenhouse, compost; community socializing zone with picnic area, BBQs, natural play area and mural. CPRS 2020 Award of Excellence.

projects.name: Hayward Plunge Renovation
projects.scope: Renovation of Hayward Plunge aquatic center: parking lot ADA access, structural repairs, concrete waterproofing, pool tiling and replastering, diving board and swim lane revisions.

projects.name: Kennedy Park Renovation
projects.scope: Design and construction per master plan: renovated picnic areas, group picnic shelters, new central play areas, new teacup amusement ride, new concession building and public restrooms, improved pathways, informal lawn areas, 39 net new trees. Park reopened April 2022. Award Winner.

projects.name: Kenneth Aitken Senior & Community Center Renovation
projects.scope: Roof and gutter replacement, new HVAC, energy-efficient lighting, accessible restrooms, site access and parking improvements, new flooring and paint. Re-opening event December 1, 2023.

projects.name: La Vista Park
projects.scope: New community park managed by City of Hayward; HARD will operate upon completion. Features: nature play areas, basketball court, picnic areas, multi-use lawn/soccer field, parking, restrooms, walking paths, Foothill Trail segment, exercise stations, off-leash dog area, and Hayward's first bike terrain park. Construction contract awarded 2025.

projects.name: Laurel School Acquisition & Future Neighborhood Park
projects.scope: Acquisition of ~3.1 acres from Hayward USD (acquired December 2025). Northern field to be converted to a public neighborhood park.

projects.name: Alameda County Agricultural History Center
projects.scope: Restoration of historic orchard and creation of agricultural demonstration area at Meek Estate Park. New interpretive signs for self-guided tour.

projects.name: Meek Estate Parking Lot
projects.scope: Renovation and expansion of existing parking lot at Meek Estate Park. Funded by Measure WW. Completed and reopened Summer 2020.

projects.name: Memorial Park Restroom Replacement
projects.scope: Demolition of existing restroom and installation of new public restroom building and ADA access improvements.

projects.name: Mission Hills Driving Range Renovation
projects.scope: Replacement of synthetic turf surfacing and vertical mesh netting, damaged paving repair, maintenance access gate replacement, and conversion of lights to LED.

projects.name: Mt. Eden Park Renovation
projects.scope: Sports field/court lighting upgrades, pavement repairs, fitness court, court resurfacing, pathway enhancements. New playground equipment installed 2025.

projects.name: Palma Ceia Park Renovation
projects.scope: Walkway upgrades with enhanced drainage, new picnic furnishings, barbecues, drinking fountains, repaved walking paths, expanded play area with new playground equipment.

projects.name: Palomares Hills Park Renovation
projects.scope: Slope stabilization, water runoff control, and pathway reconstruction and paving.

projects.name: San Lorenzo Community Park - Phase 1
projects.scope: Phase 1 construction focused on new pond area, active play zone, and softball complex. Park re-opened November 14, 2017.

projects.name: San Lorenzo Community Park - Phase 2
projects.scope: Phase 2 of 31-acre community park. Includes: multi-purpose field, two soccer fields, concessions building, dog park, community green, neighborhood play area, additional picnic facilities, exercise stations, and parking. Completed 2023.

projects.name: San Lorenzo Community Center Renovation
projects.scope: Renovation of existing 8,236 sq ft community center and surrounding site improvements. Design by Dahlin Group Architects.

projects.name: Sorensdale Park Renovation
projects.scope: Parking lot repaving, walkway improvements, grading and drainage enhancements, new picnic furnishings, BBQs, drinking fountains, improved access pathways, new tree plantings.

projects.name: Southgate Park Renovation
projects.scope: Drainage improvements, new picnic furnishings and BBQs, repaved walking paths, new playground equipment, two new picnic areas. Two tennis courts converted to six pickleball courts.

projects.name: Tennis Court Renovations (Phase 1 / Pickleball Conversions)
projects.scope: Castro Valley Community Park: 3 tennis courts renovated. Southgate Park: 2 tennis courts converted to 6 pickleball courts.

projects.name: Outdoor Fitness Courts
projects.scope: Three free outdoor fitness courts at Eden Greenway, Tennyson Park, and Alden E. Oliver Sports Park. Each court supports 28 simultaneous users on 30 equipment pieces.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 1
projects.scope: Renovations to 17 sports courts at Alden E. Oliver, Weekes, Bay Trees, and Stonybrook Parks. Basketball, tennis, pickleball, and flexible multi-use court upgrades.

projects.name: Playground Renovations (District-Wide)
projects.scope: Renovation of playgrounds at multiple sites district-wide, including Bechtel Mini Park, Birchfield Park, Castro Valley Community Park, Cherryland Park, Del Ray Park, Eden Greenway at Berry, Eldridge Park, Greenridge Park, Hillcrest Knolls Park, Ruus Park, Stonybrook Park.

projects.name: Discovery Center at Sulphur Creek Nature Center
projects.scope: Complete redesign of the Discovery Center. Design team: Noll & Tam Architects, Studio Terpeluk (Exhibit Design), Weldon Design (Interpretive Design). Funded in part by $250,000 Martins Family Foundation grant.

projects.name: 1797 D Street Acquisition
projects.scope: Acquisition of 1.43-acre site adjacent to Sulphur Creek Nature Center to expand the park. Purchase completed 2025.

projects.name: Sulphur Creek Nature Center Renovation
projects.scope: Grounds enhancements per 2021 community-led Master Plan: outdoor education area, rustic shade structure, amphitheater from reclaimed trees, interpretive/wayfinding signage, welcome kiosk, updated seating, and pollinator garden.

projects.name: Sunset Futsal Courts
projects.scope: 4 youth futsal courts, perimeter netting, lighting, restroom, drinking fountain, covered entry, spectator viewing areas. Opened February 24, 2023.

projects.name: Tennyson Park Renovation
projects.scope: Renovation of existing 10-acre park: renovated sports field and skate park, basketball courts, walking paths, pump track, picnic areas, parking, and outdoor fitness court. Re-opening event September 19, 2025.

projects.name: Mia's Dream Come True All-Access Playground
projects.scope: 1-acre all-access playground for inclusive play at Tennyson Park. Features: Music Therapy Zone, Kindness Arches, Fire Engine, Water Tower, swings, spinners, slides, fog sprays, accessible pathways, picnic areas, quiet reading area, art sculptures, new restroom. Engineering Excellence Award Winner.

projects.name: Outdoor Fitness Courts
projects.scope: Three free outdoor fitness courts at Eden Greenway, Tennyson Park, and Alden E. Oliver Sports Park. Each court supports 28 simultaneous users on 30 equipment pieces.

projects.name: Unity Park at Mission & Mattox
projects.scope: Acquisition and development of 2.6 acres at NE corner of Mission Blvd & Mattox Road. Features: pickleball courts, basketball court, multi-use court, bike skills course, play area, off-street parking, food truck area. Opening event August 12, 2025.

projects.name: Valley View Park
projects.scope: New 24-acre park with sweeping views. Master Plan includes parking, tot lot, adventure hill, multi-purpose grass area, restroom, picnic areas, terraced seating, overlooks, reflection grove, pump track, wellness areas, dog area, large loop path, and trails.

projects.name: Via Toledo Park
projects.scope: Design and construction of new 2-acre park: multipurpose lawn, half-court basketball, play area, fitness stations, picnic areas, plaza and shade structure, pathways and seating.

projects.name: Basketball & Tennis Court Renovations - Phase 1
projects.scope: Renovations to 17 sports courts at Alden E. Oliver, Weekes, Bay Trees, and Stonybrook Parks. Basketball, tennis, pickleball, and flexible multi-use court upgrades.

projects.name: Picnic Area Upgrades - Castro Valley & Weekes Parks
projects.scope: Renovation of six group picnic facilities at Castro Valley Community Park and four at Weekes Community Park. New paving, signage, trees, waste receptacles, picnic tables, benches, and BBQ grills.

projects.name: Weekes Community Park Renovation
projects.scope: Master Plan: open lawn areas, restrooms, concession building, playground, half-court basketball, bocce courts, fitness plaza, central plaza, group picnic areas, pavilion, shade structure, bandstand, promenade and walking loop.
